title: campanula grandis subsp. rizeensis
intro: Campanula grandis subsp. rizeensis, also known as the Large Bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family Campanulaceae. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Large Bellflower has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Campanula grandis subsp. rizeensis are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
